compiled_state.title: As a project maintainer, I can run lint, test, and review checks on every PR via a staged CI workflow
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A GitHub Actions CI workflow template with 3 needs:-chained jobs (lint/format, tests, LLM review placeholder) that runs on every PR to main.
**Why**: This is the foundation for CI enforcement — all other stories build on this workflow structure.
**Scope**:
- IN: CI workflow file template, 3 staged jobs with needs: dependencies, project-agnostic command resolution, placeholder review job
- OUT: Actual LLM review integration (Story 3), Ruleset configuration (Story 2), onboarding integration (Story 4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a CI workflow template exists that defines 3 jobs (lint/format, tests, review) with correct needs: dependencies so expensive jobs only run when prior jobs pass
  ← Satisfies: "CI runs checks as separate GitHub Actions jobs with needs: dependencies"
- When this story is complete, the lint/format and test jobs resolve their commands from project configuration without requiring DSO plugin scripts in the CI runner
  ← Satisfies: "CI runs checks as separate GitHub Actions jobs with needs: dependencies"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Consumer Impact] merge-to-main.sh and ci-status.sh read ci.workflow_name from dso-config.conf for CI polling and triggering. The workflow name must be coordinated with these consumers.
- [Portability] The CI workflow runs in GitHub Actions where DSO plugin scripts (read-config.sh, CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT) are not available. Command resolution must use a portable mechanism (e.g., workflow inputs, GitHub Variables, or inline config parsing) rather than depending on plugin infrastructure.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.

comments.body: Done definition update (2026-04-26 scope expansion, epic 3aaa-0238 SC8): Add a separate `validate.sh --ci` Actions job with `needs: tests` (parallel with LLM review) to the staged CI workflow. Job exits non-zero when validate.sh --ci fails. Coordinate check-context name with Ruleset story fc8b-aab0.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Consideration (adversarial review): [Consumer Impact] `validate.sh --ci` historically runs DSO plugin self-tests (skill-ref checks, contract-schema checks, hook lint) that are meaningful only when `plugins/dso/` is present in the repo. Host projects consume DSO via the marketplace, not as source. Without scoping, validate-ci becomes either vacuously passing or always failing on host projects. Verify the d025-87d4 'commands resolve without DSO plugin in CI runner' constraint applies specifically to the validate-ci job; document which subset of validate.sh checks are meaningful in host-project CI vs. plugin-repo CI.
comments.author: Test
